creator: kevans@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Steve Wills from comment #5)

Alright, I can't fix it after all. =p However- it builds fine on stable/10 (which is what I was mistakenly using for build-testing as of the last update) and beyond. Would it suffice to just mark it BROKEN for OPSYS == "FreeBSD", OSVERSION < 1004000 and let it naturally fix itself on 10.* as 10.4 releases?

If not, I'll consult with dim@ to see if he knows when it might've been fixed -- I think this is a libc++ thing, and there's only so many commits to stable/10's libc++ since releng/10.3 was branched at r281149.
